title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
core_claim: Factual recall in LLMs appears to follow a sigmoid relationship with a log-linear combination of model size and topic frequency in training data, with those variables explaining a substantial share of variance across models and families.
triage_rationale: The abstract proposes a concrete scaling relationship for factual recall and reports evaluation across many models and references, which makes it methodologically interesting and potentially useful for comparison. It is also likely to be a good episode topic because the claim is simple, testable, and directly relevant to LLM behavior, even though the evidence is only from the abstract at this stage.
likely_red_flags: Automated reference verification may introduce measurement noise or systematic bias.
The claims depend on topic-frequency estimates from training data, which may be hard to validate and may confound model family differences.
High explanatory variance within families may not generalize across architectures or data regimes.
